Title: A substrate-multiplexed platform for profiling enzymatic potential of plant family 1 glycosyltransferases

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: E. coli lysate expression system for in vitro enzyme assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant development, defense, communication; potential therapeutic relevance of glycosides
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant family 1 glycosyltransferase (UGT), GT-B fold, PSPG motif-containing enzyme
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli for lysate and purified enzyme assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows unprecedented broad sugar-acceptor promiscuity and catalyzes UDP-glucose-dependent glycosylation of numerous natural products
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73C5 was the most promiscuous enzyme in the screen, glycosylating up to 127 substrates from 29 compound superclasses. 2. Twenty-five of these substrates from 17 superclasses were validated with purified enzyme assays. 3. Structural analysis suggested a large ~1600 Å^3 acceptor pocket likely underlies broad substrate scope.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: lysate-based multiplex LC-MS/MS screen; purified GST-fusion enzyme assays; structural modeling from AlphaFold; cavity analysis
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7-based expression from pET28a and pGEX-6P-1 in E. coli (inducible by IPTG)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro reactions with UDP-glucose; lysate assays at pH 6.8, 30°C overnight; purified enzyme assays at pH 7.6, 25°C
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: mass spectrometry dataset; raw MS files at Zenodo 15670423
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study emphasizes biochemical potential rather than in planta physiological function or native metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Regioselectivity not resolved; in vivo relevance not established; activity depends partly on heterologous expression and assay conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising scaffold for enzyme engineering, biocatalysis, glycodiversification, and metabolic engineering because of exceptionally broad substrate range

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: E. coli lysate expression system for in vitro enzyme assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cytokinin N-glucosides and other O-/N-glycosylated products
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: glycosylation contributes to storage, transport, detoxification, sequestration; cytokinin conjugation affects hormone metabolism
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant family 1 glycosyltransferase with non-canonical Cys-Asp catalytic dyad
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli for lysate and purified enzyme assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a bifunctional O-/N-glycosyltransferase with strong preference for N-glycosylation, especially for cytokinin-type substrates
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76C2 with a non-canonical Cys-Asp dyad retained O-glycosylation activity despite lacking catalytic His. 2. It strongly favored N-glycosylation of 6-benzylaminopurine and 6-dimethylallylaminopurine. 3. C20H mutation increased O-glycosylation relative to wild type, while alanine mutations in C20 or D112 reduced N-glycosylation and increased O-glycosylation, supporting a role for the Cys-Asp dyad in N-glycosylation selectivity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: lysate-based LC-MS/MS screen; purified enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; semi-quantitative LC-MS; structural modeling; docking
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7-based expression from pET28a and pGEX-6P-1 in E. coli (inducible by IPTG)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro reactions with UDP-glucose at 25°C; comparison of WT and active-site mutants on O- and N-glycosylation substrates
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: mass spectrometry dataset; raw MS files at Zenodo 15670423
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported to be responsible for N-glycosylation of cytokinins in Arabidopsis; mechanistic basis may involve proximity/orientation effects rather than canonical base catalysis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Physiological in planta substrate spectrum not established here; regio/chemoselectivity mechanism remains unresolved
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful catalyst for engineering selective N-glycosylation of purine/hormone-like compounds and for studying non-canonical GT mechanisms

Common_Genes.Applied_Species: E. coli lysate expression system for in vitro enzyme assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: N-glycosylated purine/cytokinin-like products and other O-/N-glycosylated products
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: glycosylation contributes to storage, transport, detoxification, sequestration
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant family 1 glycosyltransferase with non-canonical Cys-Asp catalytic dyad
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologously expressed in E. coli for lysate and purified enzyme assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a bifunctional O-/N-glycosyltransferase with strong N-glycosylation selectivity conferred by its non-canonical Cys-Asp dyad
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76C3 retained O-glycosylation activity despite possessing Cys instead of canonical catalytic His. 2. It produced strong N-glycosylation products with cytokinin-like substrates. 3. C19H mutation shifted activity toward O-glycosylation, while C19A and D117A reduced N-glycosylation and relatively increased O-glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: lysate-based LC-MS/MS screen; purified enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; semi-quantitative LC-MS; structural modeling
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7-based expression from pET28a and pGEX-6P-1 in E. coli (inducible by IPTG)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in vitro reactions with UDP-glucose at 25°C; WT versus catalytic mutants
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: mass spectrometry dataset; raw MS files at Zenodo 15670423
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Along with UGT76C2, supports existence of non-canonical catalytic solution for N-glycosylation in plant family 1 UGTs.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native physiological roles and metabolite consequences in planta not directly established in this paper
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering selective N-glycosylation biocatalysts and probing GT catalytic diversity
